reasoning: Provide a concise summary of the article’s key points: Labour’s stance on Brexit, the planned amendments for a meaningful vote and EU nationals’ status, the rebel MPs and potential shadow cabinet changes, plus related reactions.
output: Labour said it would not frustrate Brexit and would seek amendments to secure a meaningful vote on the final deal and to protect the status of EU nationals. Ten frontbench MPs rebelled last week by backing the bill against the party line. Shadow chancellor John McDonnell warned that any frontbenchers who defied the whip again would have to step down, and Jeremy Corbyn indicated changes to his shadow cabinet would be announced in the coming days.
